mission.title: Install and Configure Metallb on Kubernetes
mission.description: MetalLB is a network load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. Installing MetalLB allows you to expose services externally in a Kubernetes environment that does not have a cloud provider's load balancer.

steps.title: Add the MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
Install MetalLB in the `metallb-system` namespace with the specified version:
```bash
helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace --version v0.15.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the MetalLB pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace metallb-system
```

steps.title: Configure MetalLB with IP Address Pool
steps.description:
Create a ConfigMap to define the IP address pool for MetalLB. Replace `<YOUR_IP_RANGE>` with your desired IP range:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - <YOUR_IP_RANGE>
EOF
```

steps.title: Check MetalLB service
steps.description:
Ensure that MetalLB is properly configured and can allocate IPs by checking the service:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace metallb-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of MetalLB will show the MetalLB pods running in the `metallb-system` namespace, and you will be able to configure IP address pools for load balancing. You can verify the service allocation by checking the services in the namespace.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo update
helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace --version v0.15.3

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - <YOUR_IP_RANGE>
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your machine.
